backlog.title: Adversarial QA Bugfix Pass
backlog.description: Remediate the 5 issues discovered during the end-to-end adversarial QA validation pass against the original PRD (1 Critical, 1 Major, 3 Minor). Each fix is localized to 1-2 script files with a TDD regression test that FAILS today and PASSES after the fix. The shipped tests/run.sh suite (24/24 passing) continues to pass throughout. No architectural changes are required — all fixes operate within the existing data-flow of activate -> input -> render -> preview -> restore.

milestones.title: Critical & Major Correctness Fixes
milestones.description: Fix the two user-visible correctness bugs: (1) the duplicate restore.sh keep-window call that permanently destroys custom status-format on window-mode confirm (Critical), and (2) the live preview not following the highlighted candidate when typing or backspacing (Major). These are the highest-priority fixes because they cause silent data loss and contradict the README's explicit promises.

tasks.title: Remove duplicate restore.sh keep-window call in window-mode confirm (Issue 1)
tasks.description: Delete the stray duplicate restore.sh keep-window line in the window-mode confirm branch of input-handler.sh so the picker tears down exactly once, matching the session-mode confirm and cancel paths. Add a regression test that sets custom status-format[0..3], performs a window-mode confirm, and asserts each index round-trips byte-identical — closing the gap left by the cancel-only M2 test.

subtasks.title: Delete duplicate restore.sh keep-window call and add window-mode confirm status-format regression test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_fd5d622d3939/bugfix/001_15442f34c366/architecture/bugfix_findings.md §ISSUE 1. The duplicate call is at scripts/input-handler.sh lines 300-301 inside the confirm action's window-mode branch (if [ "$pick_type" = "window" ]). Line 300 is the legitimate first call; line 301 is a stray paste-duplicate with an extra tab of indent. There is NO return/fi/;; between them — both execute sequentially. The first call's STEP 6 clear_all_state (scripts/state.sh) unsets every @livepicker-orig-* saved-state key; the second call's STEP 4 state_status_format_restore() then reads empty indices -> tmux set-option -gu status-format wipes every custom index, and status/renumber-windows/key-table are forced to their defaults (on/on/root). The session-mode confirm path (_confirm_land_on_session at input-handler.sh:62-118) calls restore.sh keep exactly ONCE (line 117) — no duplication.
2. INPUT: No external data needed. The fix is a one-line deletion. The test reuses the existing harness: tests/setup_socket.sh (setup_test/attach_test_client), tests/helpers.sh (fail/assert_eq), the LIVEPICKER_SCRIPTS env var, and TEST_DRIVER_SESSION fixture. The test pattern to mirror is test_restore_preserves_custom_status_format_low_indices in tests/test_restore.sh (lines ~104-137) — it sets custom status-format[0..3], activates, cancels, and asserts round-trip. The NEW test must mirror this but use @livepicker-type window and perform a window-mode CONFIRM (not cancel).
3. LOGIC: (a) In scripts/input-handler.sh, DELETE line 301 (the duplicate '"$CURRENT_DIR/restore.sh" keep-window' that has the extra tab indent). Keep line 300 (the correct single call). Do NOT change any other logic — the unlink/switch-client/select-window above and the keep-window mode arg are all correct. (b) Add a new test function test_window_confirm_preserves_custom_status_format to tests/test_restore.sh (or tests/test_create.sh — follow whichever file already imports the restore patterns; test_restore.sh is preferred). The test must: call attach_test_client; set tmux set-option -g @livepicker-type window; set genuine user overrides at status-format[0..3] (mirror the existing test's #[fg=red]custom-zero etc.); snapshot them (sfN_b); give the alpha session a 2nd window (tmux new-window -t alpha -n chosenwin); run "$LIVEPICKER_SCRIPTS/livepicker.sh" to activate; type a filter matching alpha (e.g. for c in a l p h a; do "$LIVEPICKER_SCRIPTS/input-handler.sh" type "$c"; done); run "$LIVEPICKER_SCRIPTS/input-handler.sh" confirm; then read sfN_a and assert each of sf0_a..sf3_a equals sf0_b..sf3_b via assert_eq. This test FAILS today (the duplicate wipes the overrides) and PASSES after the fix.
4. OUTPUT: The window-mode confirm branch in input-handler.sh calls restore.sh keep-window exactly once. A passing regression test proving status-format[0..3] survive a window-mode confirm.
5. MOCKING: No external services. The test runs against the isolated -L tmux socket provided by setup_test (never the user's real server). The scripts under test are the REAL scripts (not mocks).
6. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (the fix restores already-documented expected behavior: 'byte-exact restore'). The inline code comment at the restore call site may optionally be updated to note it must be called exactly once, but this is not required.

tasks.title: Sync live preview to follow highlighted candidate on type/backspace/cancel-clear (Issue 2)
tasks.description: After updating the filter and resetting the index in the type, backspace, and cancel (query-clear) actions of input-handler.sh, resolve the top filtered match and call preview.sh so the live preview area stays in sync with the status-line highlight. This reconciles PRD §3 (user story: 'the preview follows the top match') and the README ('The preview follows live') with the implementation, fixing the desync where typing moves the highlight but freezes the preview pane.

subtasks.title: Add shared preview-sync helper and wire it into type/backspace/cancel-clear branches with regression test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_fd5d622d3939/bugfix/001_15442f34c366/architecture/bugfix_findings.md §ISSUE 2. In scripts/input-handler.sh, the type (lines 127-152), backspace (159-184), and cancel query-clear (339-359) actions all set @livepicker-filter/@livepicker-index=0 and call tmux refresh-client -S, but NONE call preview.sh. Only next-session (line 216) and prev-session (line 235) resolve the top filtered match and call '"$CURRENT_DIR/preview.sh" "$target" 2>/dev/null || true'. The nav resolution pattern uses lp_build_filtered from scripts/filter.sh (the SAME function renderer.sh uses, so filtered[0] is guaranteed to be the session the status bar highlights). preview.sh (scripts/preview.sh) links the candidate's window into the driver and tracks @livepicker-linked-id; when the candidate is the self-session it clears linked_id (the empty-self-session path). The existing backspace comment at line ~167 explicitly documents this as 'a known minor UX gap' — that comment must be updated.
2. INPUT: The filter value just written to STATE_FILTER (new_filter for type, ${cur_filter%?} for backspace, "" for cancel-clear). The list from STATE_LIST. Both available via get_state. The shared filter function lp_build_filtered (already sourced from filter.sh). preview.sh at $CURRENT_DIR/preview.sh.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add a shared helper function _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match() near _confirm_land_on_session (around input-handler.sh:60) that: reads STATE_LIST and STATE_FILTER; runs mapfile -t _sync_filtered < <(lp_build_filtered "$list" "$filt"); if the array is non-empty, calls '"$CURRENT_DIR/preview.sh" "${_sync_filtered[0]}" 2>/dev/null || true' (index 0 because type/backspace/cancel-clear always reset index to 0). If the array is empty (no matches), skip the preview call (leave the prior preview as-is, mirroring nav's [ "$L" -eq 0 ] && return 0 guard). (b) Call _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match in the type branch AFTER set_state "$STATE_INDEX" "0" and BEFORE tmux refresh-client -S. (c) Call it in the backspace branch in the same position. (d) Call it in the cancel query-clear branch (the if [ -n "$cur_filter" ] path) in the same position — after set_state INDEX 0, before refresh. For cancel-clear, the filter was just set to "" so the full list is the filtered list and the top match is the first session — this correctly re-syncs to the unfiltered top. (e) Update the stale inline comments: the backspace comment at ~line 167 that says 'It does NOT call preview.sh (FINDING 4) — the top match is already shown; ... a known minor UX gap' must be revised to document that the preview now syncs to the top match. Update the type branch comment similarly if it references the gap. (f) Add regression test test_preview_follows_type_filter to tests/test_functional.sh (or tests/test_preview.sh). The test must: attach_test_client; create sessions syslog and blog (tmux new-session -d -s syslog -x 120 -y 40; tmux new-session -d -s blog -x 120 -y 40); run "$LIVEPICKER_SCRIPTS/livepicker.sh"; type a filter matching blog (for c in b l o g; do "$LIVEPICKER_SCRIPTS/input-handler.sh" type "$c"; done); assert that @livepicker-linked-id is NON-EMPTY and equals blog's active window id (read via tmux list-windows -t '=blog' -F '#{window_id}' -f '#{window_active}'). Before the fix, linked-id stays "" (self-session preview) so the test FAILS. Also add a variant: type filter, then backspace to clear it, and assert linked-id re-syncs back to the first session in the list (or self-session). Mirror the preview test patterns in tests/test_preview.sh (lp_preview_seed_state is NOT needed since livepicker.sh activate seeds state; the test drives the full activate->type flow).
4. OUTPUT: _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match helper defined in input-handler.sh. Called in type, backspace, and cancel query-clear branches. A passing regression test proving @livepicker-linked-id tracks the top filtered match on type.
5. MOCKING: No external services. The test runs against the isolated -L tmux socket. The scripts under test are the REAL scripts. preview.sh is called directly (not mocked).
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the inline code comments in input-handler.sh type/backspace/cancel branches that document the 'known minor UX gap' (especially line ~167) to reflect the fixed preview-syncs-to-highlight behavior. The README already promises 'The preview follows live' (Usage §3) and 'a live, all-panes preview of the highlighted candidate' (Overview) — these now match the implementation, so no README change is needed for Mode A here (the cross-cutting README/CHANGELOG sync is the final Mode B task).

milestones.title: Minor Robustness Fixes
milestones.description: Fix the three minor issues: (3) renderer # injection into tmux format strings, (4) window-mode preview showing the active window instead of the highlighted one, and (5) README referencing a non-existent ./validate.sh. These are robustness/UX/docs improvements that do not cause data loss but degrade the picker experience or mislead users.

tasks.title: Escape # in renderer format strings to prevent tmux format injection (Issue 3)
tasks.description: Escape every # to ## (tmux's literal-# escape) when emitting session/window names and the filter query into #[...] format strings in renderer.sh. This prevents tmux from interpreting # in candidate names (e.g. #dev -> day-of-month expansion) or injecting styles (e.g. #[fg=red]). The escape is render-only — stored state (filter.sh output, @livepicker-list) stays pristine so confirm/select target the real name.

subtasks.title: Add #->## escaping for candidate names and filter query in renderer with regression test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_fd5d622d3939/bugfix/001_15442f34c366/architecture/bugfix_findings.md §ISSUE 3 and architecture/bugfix_findings.md §External tmux research. In scripts/renderer.sh, user-controlled strings are emitted raw into #[...] format strings at 5 sites: line 78 (out=...query> $FILTER (no match) 0/$TOTAL...), line 80 (out=...query> $FILTER (no match)...), line 94 (seg="#[fg=$HFG,bg=$HBG]${filtered[$i]}#[default]"), line 96 (seg="#[fg=$FG,bg=$BG]${filtered[$i]}#[default]"), line 107 (out=...query> $FILTER [$((cidx+1))/$FLEN]...). tmux treats # as a format-specifier introducer (#d=day, #S=session, #[...]=style, ##=literal #). A name like #dev renders as <day-of-month>ev. The ONLY escape for a literal # is doubling to ##. filter.sh (lp_build_filtered) correctly preserves original bytes for matching and confirm resolution — do NOT change filter.sh. The escape must happen at emission in renderer.sh ONLY.
2. INPUT: The filtered[] array (candidate names from lp_build_filtered) and $FILTER (the live query from get_state STATE_FILTER). Both are raw strings that may contain #.
3. LOGIC: (a) In scripts/renderer.sh, BEFORE building the segment string in the render loop, escape each candidate name: add a local variable e.g. `local esc_name` and compute `esc_name="${filtered[$i]//\#/##}"` (bash parameter expansion: replace every # with ##). Use $esc_name instead of ${filtered[$i]} in lines 94 and 96. (b) Escape $FILTER for the query display: add `local esc_filter` and compute `esc_filter="${FILTER//\#/##}"`. Use $esc_filter instead of $FILTER in lines 78, 80, and 107. (c) Do NOT escape the literal format constructs the code authors itself (#[fg=...], #[default], $FG/$BG/$HFG/$HBG color values, $TOTAL, $FLEN, the $((cidx+1)) arithmetic) — only the user-derived strings. (d) Add regression test test_renderer_escapes_hash_in_names to tests/test_functional.sh (or a new tests/test_renderer.sh that is auto-discovered by run.sh). The test must: setup_test; create a session with # in its name (tmux new-session -d -s '#dev'); set @livepicker-list to '#dev' and @livepicker-filter '' and @livepicker-index '0' (seed minimal state the renderer reads — mirror lp_preview_seed_state pattern but for renderer: just set the @livepicker-* options directly); run scripts/renderer.sh and capture its stdout; assert the output contains '##dev' (the escaped form) and does NOT contain '#dev' as a bare unescaped sequence (use assert_contains for '##dev'). Before the fix, the renderer emits '#dev' raw. NOTE: renderer.sh sources options.sh + utils.sh + state.sh + filter.sh internally, so it can be run standalone on the isolated socket.
4. OUTPUT: renderer.sh escapes # to ## at all 5 emission sites. A passing regression test proving a name containing # is emitted as ##.
5. MOCKING: No external services. The test runs on the isolated -L tmux socket. renderer.sh reads @livepicker-* options directly (it is client-independent — no attach_test_client needed).
6. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (renderer internal; the rendered output is visually identical for names without #).

tasks.title: Fix window-mode preview to show the highlighted window, not the session's active window (Issue 4)
tasks.description: In preview.sh, when @livepicker-type is window, parse the session:window_index token and resolve the specific window at that index (by @id), rather than always resolving the session's active window via #{window_active}. The existing link/select-by-@id flow then correctly previews the highlighted window.

subtasks.title: Add window-index-aware resolution in preview.sh for window mode with regression test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_fd5d622d3939/bugfix/001_15442f34c366/architecture/bugfix_findings.md §ISSUE 4. In scripts/preview.sh line 115, src_id is resolved via `tmux list-windows -t "=$S" -F '#{window_id}' -f '#{window_active}'` which ALWAYS returns the session's active window. In window mode, $S is a session:window_index token (from livepicker.sh:103: `list-windows -a -F '#{session_name}:#{window_index}'`), but the #{window_active} filter ignores the index. preview.sh already sources options.sh (line 38) so opt_type() is available (options.sh:27: returns 'session' or 'window'). The confirm branch in input-handler.sh ALREADY splits the token correctly: `w_sess="${target%%:*}"` and uses `select-window -t "$target"`. The rest of preview.sh (duplicate guard at line ~125, unlink at ~132, link-window at ~138, select-window at ~146, set_state STATE_LINKED_ID at ~149) operates on $src_id (@id handle) and works unchanged. IMPORTANT from external research: `-f '#{window_index} == 5'` does NOT filter (expands to non-empty text for every window -> always truthy). Must resolve by listing all windows and matching the index, or use target syntax. renumber-windows is ON during the picker but LIST is snapshotted at activation (stable for picker lifetime). Address windows by @id (the plugin's invariant — never index).
2. INPUT: $S (argv[1]) — in window mode this is 'session_name:window_index' (e.g. 'alpha:2'); in session mode this is a bare session name. The mode via opt_type(). preview.sh already sources options.sh + utils.sh + state.sh.
3. LOGIC: (a) In scripts/preview.sh preview_main(), BEFORE the existing src_id resolution (line ~115), add a branch: `local w_sess w_idx`; if [ "$(opt_type)" = "window" ] AND $S contains a ':' (i.e. it is a token), parse `w_sess="${S%%:*}"` and `w_idx="${S#*:}"`. Then resolve the specific window: `src_id="$(tmux list-windows -t "=$w_sess" -F '#{window_id}:#{window_index}' 2>/dev/null | awk -F: -v idx="$w_idx" '$2==idx {print $1; exit}')"`. This lists all windows in the session, matches the one whose index equals w_idx, and returns its @id. If src_id is empty (window gone/race), fall through to the existing preview_fallback. (b) If opt_type is NOT window (session mode), keep the EXISTING line 115 unchanged: `src_id="$(tmux list-windows -t "=$S" -F '#{window_id}' -f '#{window_active}' 2>/dev/null)"`. (c) Also update preview_fallback for window mode: if called with a session:index token in window mode, the capture-pane target should be "=$w_sess:$w_idx." (the active pane of that specific window) rather than "=$1:." which may target the wrong window. Parse the same way or pass the already-parsed w_sess:w_idx. (d) Add regression test test_window_preview_shows_highlighted_window to tests/test_preview.sh. The test must: lp_preview_seed_state; set @livepicker-type window; create a session 'multi' with TWO windows (tmux new-session -d -s multi -x 120 -y 40; tmux new-window -t multi -n secondwin); determine the index and @id of the SECOND (non-active) window (via list-windows -t '=multi' -F '#{window_index}:#{window_id}'); call preview.sh 'multi:<second_window_index>'; assert @livepicker-linked-id equals the second window's @id (NOT the active window's @id). Before the fix, linked-id is the active window's @id (or empty if the link fails). NOTE: this test exercises preview.sh directly (preview.sh is client-independent — reads @livepicker-orig-session from state, not display-message).
4. OUTPUT: preview.sh resolves the specific highlighted window by index in window mode. Session mode unchanged. A passing regression test proving the linked window is the highlighted one, not the active one.
5. MOCKING: No external services. The test runs on the isolated -L tmux socket. preview.sh is called directly (client-independent).
6. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (preview internal; the preview behavior improves for window mode but no option/config changes).

tasks.title: Fix README reference to non-existent ./validate.sh (Issue 5)
tasks.description: Remove the README sentence that advertises a VALIDATE_SKIP_SLOW=1 budget in ./validate.sh, which does not exist. The real and only test entry point is 'bash tests/run.sh' (already documented on the preceding line).

subtasks.title: Remove the non-existent validate.sh reference from README Validation section
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_fd5d622d3939/bugfix/001_15442f34c366/architecture/bugfix_findings.md §ISSUE 5. README.md line 182 (inside the ## Validation section, which starts at line 172) contains the sentence: 'a `VALIDATE_SKIP_SLOW=1` budget is available in `./validate.sh` for faster static + E2E checks.' Confirmed: NO validate.sh exists anywhere in the repo (find . -name 'validate.sh' returns empty). tests/run.sh has no VALIDATE_SKIP_SLOW handling. Users following the README get 'No such file or directory.' The real entry point 'bash tests/run.sh' is already documented on README.md line 174. Neither validate.sh nor VALIDATE_SKIP_SLOW is referenced anywhere else in the codebase.
2. INPUT: README.md current content at the Validation section (lines 172-189). No code changes needed.
3. LOGIC: In README.md, remove the trailing clause on line 182 from 'a `VALIDATE_SKIP_SLOW=1` budget is available in `./validate.sh` for faster static + E2E checks. ' so that the paragraph reads: '...each test starts a fresh isolated tmux server and sources the user config). The suites cover the PRD §15 clusters:'. Do NOT remove or change the 'bash tests/run.sh' command (line 174) — it is correct. Do NOT add a validate.sh wrapper (the PRD's suggested fix offers two options; the doc-removal is in-scope per the bugfix SOW; shipping a new wrapper is a feature addition outside this pass).
4. OUTPUT: README.md Validation section no longer references validate.sh or VALIDATE_SKIP_SLOW.
5. MOCKING: N/A — documentation-only change.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] This subtask IS the README documentation fix. The change removes a broken reference from README.md §Validation. No other doc files reference validate.sh.

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
milestones.description: Final cross-cutting documentation sweep covering the whole changeset: update the CHANGELOG with entries for all 5 bugfixes and verify README consistency (the preview-follows-type fix now makes the README's 'The preview follows live' promise true; the validate.sh fix is already handled by P1.M2.T3.S1). This task runs LAST and depends on every implementing subtask.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (CHANGELOG + README overview)
tasks.description: Update CHANGELOG.md with entries for all 5 bugfixes from this adversarial QA pass, and verify README.md is consistent with the implemented behavior (preview now follows type, validate.sh reference removed).

subtasks.title: Update CHANGELOG with all 5 bugfix entries
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_fd5d622d3939/bugfix/001_15442f34c366/architecture/system_context.md for the bug summary table. The CHANGELOG.md at the repo root follows the existing format (examine its current structure — it has an invariants/changes pattern). All 5 issues are documented in the PRD (Bug Fix Requirements): Issue 1 (Critical: duplicate restore call destroys status-format), Issue 2 (Major: preview doesn't follow highlight on type), Issue 3 (Minor: renderer # injection), Issue 4 (Minor: window-mode preview shows active window), Issue 5 (Minor: README validate.sh reference).
2. INPUT: The completed implementations from P1.M1.T1.S1 (Issue 1 fix), P1.M1.T2.S1 (Issue 2 fix), P1.M2.T1.S1 (Issue 3 fix), P1.M2.T2.S1 (Issue 4 fix), P1.M2.T3.S1 (Issue 5 fix). All must be complete before this subtask runs.
3. LOGIC: (a) Read CHANGELOG.md to understand the existing format/structure/conventions. (b) Add a new section (or entries) for this adversarial QA bugfix pass. Document each of the 5 fixes with: severity (Critical/Major/Minor), a one-line summary, the file(s) changed, and the user-visible impact (e.g. 'Fixed: window-mode confirm no longer destroys custom status-format configuration'). Follow the existing CHANGELOG conventions for version/date headers if present; if there is no version number, add entries under an 'Unreleased' or 'Bugfix' heading consistent with the file's style. (c) Verify README.md is consistent: the 'The preview follows live' promise (Usage §3) is now true (Issue 2 fixed); the validate.sh reference is removed (Issue 5 fixed in P1.M2.T3.S1). If any other README section needs a minor touch to reflect the fixes (e.g. the Overview's 'live preview' language is now accurate), apply it.
4. OUTPUT: CHANGELOG.md updated with all 5 bugfix entries. README.md verified consistent.
5. MOCKING: N/A — documentation-only change.
6. DOCS: This IS the changeset-level documentation sync task (Mode B). It covers CHANGELOG.md and README.md overview sections that span the whole changeset. Per-subtask docs (Mode A) were handled in the implementing subtasks.
